behavior_summary: When the player uses the 'U8P_SGARGL::look' (examine) action on this gargoyle entity, the script emits a single short descriptive string/bark: "gargoyle " (note the trailing space).
Immediately after emitting that text the process issues a suspend, which in this context appears to end or pause the U8P_SGARGL::look interaction until a subsequent event.
No parameters, global flags, local variables, conditional branches, menus, or external subsystem calls are present — the behavior is purely descriptive and one-shot.
notable_control_flow: U8P_SGARGL::look is a process (processType 0x0208) that runs on examine/inspect and executes a single Bark node followed by a suspend; there are no branches or further actions.
The function contains only simple sequential flow: display text -> suspend/terminate the process.
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the bark text "gargoyle " intentional (formatting for concatenation) or an artifact of extraction?
Does suspend here strictly terminate the U8P_SGARGL::look process or merely pause it awaiting engine-driven resume? (behavior is inferred from similar scripts but not explicit)
Is this entity treated as an NPC (can be spoken to) or as a static object for UI purposes? The code is the same for both cases, but higher-level usage is unclear.

main_function.name: U8P_SGARGL::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short on-screen description when a player examines the gargoyle entity; display a single bark string and then suspend the U8P_SGARGL::look process.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the game engine when the player performs a 'U8P_SGARGL::look' or 'examine' action on the gargoyle (processType 0x0208).
Also may be invoked by any engine fast-area/interaction that routes to the object's U8P_SGARGL::look handler.

function_map.name: U8P_SGARGL::look
function_map.role: Handle the examine/U8P_SGARGL::look interaction and present a single descriptive bark string.
